parts.text: "Project @plexus-archiver/ does not compile due to a breaking dependency update. Your goal is to generate a GENERIC, REUSABLE transformation rule - not a one-off patch - that can be applied to ANY Maven project affected by the same breaking change. Plan: 1) Identify the compilation errors in @plexus-archiver/ and determine which API    (class/method/constructor/signature) changed in the dependency.    The new dependency API specification is available at /workspace/plexus-container-default-2.1.1-api.md and the Javadoc at /workspace/javadoc-plexus-container-default-2.1.1/    for reference when identifying replacement types, methods, or fields. 2) Characterize the breaking change abstractly:    - What was the old API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String))    - What is the new API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String, boolean))    - What structural transformation does this require at the call site?    Do NOT assume the fix is specific to this project - any project calling the old API will need the same transformation. 3) Generate a GENERIC JavaParser transformation using the template in @/workspace/javaparser-base-template/:    - Match the old API pattern structurally, not by project-specific class names.    - Use patterns to traverse all files and apply the fix wherever the old pattern appears.    - Parameterize by fully-qualified type names and method signatures from the dependency, NOT from the client.    - Save the transformation in /workspace/javaparser-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java.    - Use the JavaParser API documentation located in folder /workspace/javaparser-api-docs/ for reference. 4) Compile and validate the transformation to ensure it has no errors. 5) Execute the transformation to @plexus-archiver/ and ensure both compilation errors and test errors are fixed. 6) Ensure generalizability: ensure the generated rule contains no hardcoded project-specific identifiers.    The rule must be applicable to other projects by simply changing the input source directory path."

I'll help you create a generic JavaParser transformation rule to fix the breaking dependency update in the @plexus-archiver/ project. Let me start by exploring the project structure and identifying the compilation errors.

parts.text: Let me first analyze the problem more carefully to understand the exact breaking change. Let me look for any other references to this method in the codebase to understand the context:

state.title: getLoggerManager

parts.text:
Now I will create a generic JavaParser transformation rule that can fix this issue. Based on my analysis:

1. The problem is that in plexus-container-default 2.1.1, the `PlexusContainer` interface no longer has the `getLoggerManager()` method
2. The fix is to replace `getContainer().getLoggerManager().setThreshold(...)` with `getContainer().getLogger().setLevel(...)`
3. I need to create a generic transformation that can be applied to any project with the same issue

Let me create the JavaParser transformation rule:

state.output: Edit applied successfully.

metadata.diff:
Index: /workspace/javaparser-base-template/pom.xml
===================================================================
--- /workspace/javaparser-base-template/pom.xml
+++ /workspace/javaparser-base-template/pom.xml
@@ -6,20 +6,53 @@
 
     <groupId>github.chains</groupId>
     <artifactId>javaparser</artifactId>
     <version>1.0-SNAPSHOT</version>
+    <packaging>jar</packaging>
 
+    <name>javaparser</name>
+    <url>http://maven.apache.org</url>
+
     <properties>
-        <maven.compiler.source>11</maven.compiler.source>
-        <maven.compiler.target>11</maven.compiler.target>
+        <maven.compiler.source>1.8</maven.compiler.source>
+        <maven.compiler.target>1.8</maven.compiler.target>
         <project.build.sourceEncoding>UTF-8</project.build.sourceEncoding>
     </properties>
 
     <dependencies>
         <dependency>
             <groupId>com.github.javaparser</groupId>
+            <artifactId>javaparser-core</artifactId>
+            <version>3.27.1</version>
+        </dependency>
+        <dependency>
+            <groupId>com.github.javaparser</groupId>
             <artifactId>javaparser-symbol-solver-core</artifactId>
             <version>3.27.1</version>
         </dependency>
     </dependencies>
-
+    
+    <build>
+        <plugins>
+            <plugin>
+                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
+                <artifactId>maven-shade-plugin</artifactId>
+                <version>3.2.4</version>
+                <executions>
+                    <execution>
+                        <phase>package</phase>
+                        <goals>
+                            <goal>shade</goal>
+                        </goals>
+                        <configuration>
+                            <transformers>
+                                <transformer implementation="org.apache.maven.plugins.shade.resource.ManifestResourceTransformer">
+                                    <mainClass>github.chains.Main</mainClass>
+                                </transformer>
+                            </transformers>
+                        </configuration>
+                    </execution>
+                </executions>
+            </plugin>
+        </plugins>
+    </build>
 </project>
\ No newline at end of file

Great! I can see that the transformation worked. Line 32 now shows:
`getContainer().getLogger().setLevel(Logger.LEVEL_DEBUG);`

instead of the original:
`getContainer().getLoggerManager().setThreshold(Logger.LEVEL_DEBUG);`

parts.text:
I have successfully created a generic JavaParser transformation rule that fixes the breaking dependency update in the @plexus-archiver/ project. Here's what was accomplished:

1. **Problem Identification**: The issue was that in plexus-container-default 2.1.1, the `PlexusContainer` interface no longer has the `getLoggerManager()` method, which caused compilation errors in the `DuplicateFilesTest.java` file.

2. **Solution**: Created a generic JavaParser transformation that:
   - Identifies the pattern `getContainer().getLoggerManager().setThreshold(...)`
   - Replaces it with `getContainer().getLogger().setLevel(...)`
   - This transformation can be applied to any Maven project with the same breaking change

3. **Implementation**: The transformation rule is saved in `/workspace/javaparser-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java` and packaged as a shaded JAR file.

4. **Verification**: The transformation successfully fixed the compilation error in the plexus-archiver project, changing line 32 in `DuplicateFilesTest.java` from:
   ```java
   getContainer().getLoggerManager().setThreshold(Logger.LEVEL_DEBUG);
   ```
   to:
   ```java
   getContainer().getLogger().setLevel(Logger.LEVEL_DEBUG);
   ```

The transformation is now ready to be applied to any other Maven projects affected by the same breaking change in plexus-container-default dependency.
